task:
<command-message>superpowers:requesting-code-review</command-message>
<command-name>/superpowers:requesting-code-review</command-name>
<command-args>Can you thoroughly review the changes in this branch? Focus on the changes between this branch and origin/main.</command-args>
Can you add a test for this?
Can you create a thorough but concise description of the changes in this branch against origin/main? Please format it using markdown and copy it to my clipboard.
user_feedback_text: Can you create a thorough but concise description of the changes in this branch against origin/main? Please format it using markdown and copy it to my clipboard.

task: Sub-steps are sorted by Name using string comparison, but names contain numeric suffixes like process_sessions.0, process_sessions.1, etc. For 10+ iterations, lexicographic ordering places process_sessions.10 before process_sessions.2, producing incorrect display order. The comment on line 141 says "sorted by index" but the implementation sorts by string. Since the integer index is already available as the map key in subStepDurations[name] (map[int]int64), sorting numerically by index would be correct.
user_feedback_text: Sub-steps are sorted by Name using string comparison, but names contain numeric suffixes like process_sessions.0, process_sessions.1, etc. For 10+ iterations, lexicographic ordering places process_sessions.10 before process_sessions.2, producing incorrect display order. The comment on line 141 says "sorted by index" but the implementation sorts by string. Since the integer index is already available as the map key in subStepDurations[name] (map[int]int64), sorting numerically by index would be correct.

task: When the log file doesn't exist (e.g., user runs entire perf before any hooks have been invoked), this returns a raw error like "collecting perf entries: opening perf log: open .../entire.log: no such file or directory". It would be more user-friendly to check for os.IsNotExist(err) and return an empty entries list instead, which would then trigger the helpful "No perf entries found" message with instructions on enabling DEBUG logging.
user_feedback_text: When the log file doesn't exist (e.g., user runs entire perf before any hooks have been invoked), this returns a raw error like "collecting perf entries: opening perf log: open .../entire.log: no such file or directory". It would be more user-friendly to check for os.IsNotExist(err) and return an empty entries list instead, which would then trigger the helpful "No perf entries found" message with instructions on enabling DEBUG logging.
